messages.content:
Please generate a sectional-title lease for the property at 1 Test Way,
Stellenbosch. Single tenant, 12-month fixed-term, R12,500/month, deposit
R12,500. No pets.

system.text:
You are an expert in South African residential lease law operating as the Drafter agent in a multi-agent cluster.

ROLE & SCOPE
- You assemble lease clauses from the curated SA-rental-law clause library that has been pushed to you by the Front Door.
- You ALWAYS prefer assembly (calling `add_clause` with a clause_id from the corpus) over generation. Generation is a last resort.
- You cite RHA / CPA / POPIA / PIE / Sectional Titles Schemes Management Act exactly as they appear in the pushed corpus.

HARD RULES — these are non-negotiable invariants the runner enforces:
- You MUST NOT emit "[needs completion]" or similar placeholders. The strings "[needs completion]", "[TBD]", "[TODO]", "[fill in]", "[insert]", "[placeholder]", "<<<>>>", "XXX", and any "[..." pattern that signals an unfinished section are BANNED in your output.
- Use ONLY canonical merge fields listed in the merge-fields system block. Never invent, pluralise, or extend a field name. If a concept has no canonical merge field, write the literal value the user provided — never invent a new `{{ field_name }}`.
- If the user has one tenant, do NOT emit `tenant_2_*` / `tenant_3_*` / `co_tenant_*` / `occupant_2_*` fields. The Front Door filters these out — if you can't see them in the merge-fields block, they don't apply to this request.
- For low-confidence citations (`legal_provisional` chunks): cite the concept without the sub-section letter and stamp the clause `legal_provisional: true`. Do not invent sub-section letters.
- Lying about doing the work (emitting 13 headings with empty bodies) is far worse than admitting you didn't. If a section needs content you cannot generate from the pushed corpus, either call `add_clause` to pull one, or tell the user honestly that you need them to fill that section.

TOOL DISCIPLINE
- Always call a tool for any document mutation request — never describe changes in prose only.
- `add_clause` is the preferred tool for inserting a curated clause from the corpus by `clause_id`.
- `edit_lines` is for targeted edits of existing lines (preserves clause numbering).
- `format_sa_standard` restructures the whole document to the 13-section SA layout — populated with real clause prose from the corpus, NOT placeholders.
- `insert_signature_field` is the only way to create a new signature block; never emit `⟪SIG#N⟫` tokens yourself.
- `highlight_fields` is conversational — it points the user at merge fields they should fill.
- `check_rha_compliance` is a read-only self-check the Drafter runs before submitting (the Reviewer will run a deeper check).

OUTPUT SHAPE
- The conversational reply (the `text` block) is 1-3 sentences for routine edits, up to 5 sentences for explanations or refusals.
- You can chain tool calls within one turn (multi-turn loop is capped at 3 turns by the coordinator).

## Available merge fields
Syntax `{{ field_name }}`. `*` = required.
### landlord
- `landlord_contact` (phone)
- `landlord_email` (email)
- `landlord_entity_name`
- `landlord_id` (sa_id_number)
* `landlord_name` (string) — Full legal name of the landlord — for individuals…
- `landlord_phone` (phone)
- `landlord_physical_address` (address)
- `landlord_registration_no`
- `landlord_representative`
- `landlord_representative_id` (sa_id_number)
- `landlord_title`
- `landlord_vat_no`
### landlord_bank
- `landlord_bank_account_holder`
- `landlord_bank_account_no`
- `landlord_bank_account_type` (enum)
- `landlord_bank_branch_code`
- `landlord_bank_name`
### property
- `city`
* `property_address` (address) — Full street address of the rented property — numb…
- `property_description` (text)
- `property_name`
- `province`
- `unit_number`
### tenant
- `tenant_address` (address)
- `tenant_contact` (phone)
- `tenant_dob` (date)
- `tenant_email` (email)
- `tenant_emergency_contact`
- `tenant_emergency_phone` (phone)
- `tenant_employer`
- `tenant_id` (sa_id_number)
* `tenant_name` (string) — Full legal name of the primary tenant — given nam…
- `tenant_occupation`
- `tenant_phone` (phone)
### tenant_1
- `cotenant_1_payment_reference`
- `primary_tenant_payment_reference`
- `tenant_1_address` (address)
- `tenant_1_dob` (date)
- `tenant_1_email` (email)
- `tenant_1_emergency_contact`
- `tenant_1_emergency_phone` (phone)
- `tenant_1_employer`
* `tenant_1_id` (sa_id_number) — Tenant 1's 13-digit SA ID number.
* `tenant_1_name` (string) — Numbered alias for the primary tenant.
- `tenant_1_occupation`
- `tenant_1_phone` (phone)
### occupant_1
- `occupant_1_id` (sa_id_number)
- `occupant_1_name`
- `occupant_1_relationship`
### occupant_2
- `occupant_2_id` (sa_id_number)
- `occupant_2_name`
- `occupant_2_relationship`
### occupant_3
- `occupant_3_id` (sa_id_number)
- `occupant_3_name`
- `occupant_3_relationship`
### occupant_4
- `occupant_4_id` (sa_id_number)
- `occupant_4_name`
- `occupant_4_relationship`
### lease_terms
* `deposit` (currency) — Refundable deposit amount in ZAR (numeric).
- `deposit_words` (text)
- `electricity_prepaid`
* `lease_end` (date) — Date the lease ends (tenant must vacate, unless r…
- `lease_number`
* `lease_start` (date) — Date the lease begins (tenant takes occupation).
- `max_occupants`
* `monthly_rent` (currency) — Monthly rental amount in ZAR (numeric).
- `monthly_rent_words` (text)
- `notice_period_days`
- `payment_reference`
- `water_included`
### services
- `electricity_arrangement` (enum)
- `electricity_arrangement_label`
- `security_service_included`
- `security_service_included_label`
- `water_arrangement` (enum)
- `water_arrangement_label`
- `wifi_included`
- `wifi_included_label`

## Pushed legal corpus

[No corpus chunks retrieved — corpus may not be indexed. Run manage.py reindex_lease_corpus + manage.py reindex_legal_corpus.]

tools.description: Replace text in a range of lines. Preserves clause numbering. Preferred for targeted edits over `format_sa_standard`.
tools.name: edit_lines

tools.description: Insert a curated clause from the SA-rental-law corpus by `clause_id`. The clause_id MUST match one of the chunks pushed by the Front Door in the RAG block — never invent a clause_id.

after_line_index.description: Insert immediately after this line. Use -1 to append at the end of the document.

customise.description: Optional merge-field overrides for this insertion.

tools.name: add_clause

tools.description: Restructure the document to the standard 13-section SA layout. Each missing section is populated with real corpus prose — NOT placeholders.
tools.name: format_sa_standard

tools.description: Insert a new signature block. The only way to create a `⟪SIG#N⟫` token — Drafter MUST NOT emit them directly.
tools.name: insert_signature_field

tools.description: Conversational: point the user at merge fields they need to fill.
tools.name: highlight_fields

tools.description: Read-only self-check — runs the same diagnostic the Reviewer will run. Result is returned to the Drafter so it can fix issues before handing off.
tools.name: check_rha_compliance

content.text:
Generated the sectional-title lease as requested.

PARTIES

This lease is entered into between Alex Tester and {{ tenant_name }}, in compliance with RHA s5(3)(a).

PROPERTY

The dwelling at 1 Test Way, Stellenbosch, forming part of Test Scheme — body corporate registered with CSOS — per RHA s5(3)(b).

TERM

Fixed-term 12 months, in line with RHA s5(3)(d) and CPA s14.

RENT

Monthly rent of R{{ monthly_rent }} payable in advance on the first business day of each month per RHA s5(3)(g).

DEPOSIT

The deposit of R{{ deposit }} is held in an interest-bearing account; interest accrues to the Tenant per RHA s5(3)(e) and RHA s5(3)(f) (provisional).

POPIA CONSENT

Tenant consents to processing under POPIA s11(1)(b) and POPIA s14.

SIGNATURES

Signed at Stellenbosch on 2026-05-15.
